title: Prepend a comment to files setup in container
body:
setup.rpm attempts to modify /etc/hosts, if it thinks
it has never been modified.  By adding a #comment to the
front of the file, it should prevent this from blowing up
the build.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

> @mglantz Are you saying this fixes the issue or not?

@rhatdan Testing on F29, it does not fix the issue. I have the same problem as before, setup upgrade fails due to it trying to write to /etc/hosts.
@mglantz Could you try this one.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL

> @mglantz Could you try this one.

I tried it out on a fresh F29 install and I see the same problem I'm afraid. Just in case I'm messing this somehow, full test is as such:
```
cd
cat << 'EOF' >Dockerfile
FROM registry.centos.org/che-stacks/centos-stack-base
RUN sudo yum -y update setup
CMD tail -f /dev/null 
EOF
mkdir buildah
cd buildah
export GOPATH=`pwd`
git clone -b copy1 https://github.com/rhatdan/buildah ./src/github.com/containers/buildah
cd src/github.com/containers/buildah
make
sudo make install
cd
podman build -t buildah:test .
```
@mglantz Could you run a container and cat /etc/hosts file, to make sure my fix took place.
Actually you need to do this with buildah bud not with podman.  If we fix it here then we need to vendor this into podman.

> Actually you need to do this with buildah bud not with podman. If we fix it here then we need to vendor this into podman.

@rhatdan Aah. I thought podman called on buildah. Using buildah directly:

```
buildah from registry.centos.org-che-stacks/centos-stack-base
buildah run centos-stack-base-working-container-3 -- sudo yum -y update setup
```
Things works without any problem.
Fixes https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1669197
LGTM
@vrothberg one last check please and then merge?

@rh-atomic-bot r+
LGTM
